description: Fix E2E test plugin tools that use stale inline implementations instead of current SDK exports, causing the tools to not actually test what they claim to test
qualityChecks: cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use SDK removeLocalStorage/removeSessionStorage in sdkRemoveStorage tool
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the sdk_remove_storage E2E test tool to use the SDK's removeLocalStorage and removeSessionStorage exports so that E2E tests actually exercise the SDK removal utilities
acceptanceCriteria: sdkRemoveStorage tool imports removeLocalStorage and removeSessionStorage from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
The tool uses removeLocalStorage(key) for local storage removal instead of inline localStorage.removeItem(key)
The tool uses removeSessionStorage(key) for session storage removal instead of inline sessionStorage.removeItem(key)
The stale comment about the published SDK not exporting these functions is removed
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run format:check passes

userStories.title: Use SDK log export in logLevels tool instead of globalThis.__openTabs.log
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the log_levels E2E test tool to import log from the SDK's public API so that E2E tests verify the exported log interface works end-to-end
acceptanceCriteria: logLevels tool imports log from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
The SdkLog interface and getSdkLog() helper function are removed
The tool uses log.debug(), log.info(), log.warn(), and log.error() from the SDK import
The stale comment about older published versions not including the log module is removed
The tool no longer falls back to returning { ok: false, levels: [] } — if log is unavailable, it should throw or use the import directly (which is always available in the adapter IIFE)
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run format:check passes

userStories.title: Use SDK factory methods with custom codes in errorCustomCode tool
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the error_custom_code E2E test tool to use the SDK factory methods (ToolError.auth, ToolError.rateLimited, etc.) with custom code parameters so that E2E tests verify custom codes work through the factory API
acceptanceCriteria: The 'auth' case uses ToolError.auth('Custom auth error', 'CUSTOM_AUTH') instead of new ToolError(...) with opts
The 'rate_limited' case uses ToolError.rateLimited('Custom rate limit', 3000, 'CUSTOM_RATE_LIMIT') instead of new ToolError(...) with opts
The 'validation' case uses ToolError.validation('Custom validation', 'CUSTOM_VALIDATION') instead of new ToolError(...) with opts
The 'timeout' case uses ToolError.timeout('Custom timeout', 'CUSTOM_TIMEOUT') instead of new ToolError(...) with opts
The 'internal' case uses ToolError.internal('Custom internal', 'CUSTOM_INTERNAL') instead of new ToolError(...) with opts
The 'not_found' case remains unchanged (already uses the factory method)
The stale comment about SDK v0.0.16 only supporting custom codes on notFound() is removed
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/error-custom-code.ts

The stale comment on lines 8-11 says 'The published SDK (v0.0.16) only supports custom codes on notFound()'. This is wrong — all factory methods now accept an optional custom code parameter. See platform/plugin-sdk/src/errors.ts:
- ToolError.auth(message, code?) — line 41
- ToolError.notFound(message, code?) — line 46
- ToolError.rateLimited(message, retryAfterMs?, code?) — line 51
- ToolError.validation(message, code?) — line 56
- ToolError.timeout(message, code?) — line 62
- ToolError.internal(message, code?) — line 67

Replace each `new ToolError(...)` with the corresponding factory method:

```typescript
case 'auth':
  throw ToolError.auth('Custom auth error', 'CUSTOM_AUTH');
case 'not_found':
  throw ToolError.notFound('Custom not found', 'CUSTOM_NOT_FOUND');
case 'rate_limited':
  throw ToolError.rateLimited('Custom rate limit', 3000, 'CUSTOM_RATE_LIMIT');
case 'validation':
  throw ToolError.validation('Custom validation', 'CUSTOM_VALIDATION');
case 'timeout':
  throw ToolError.timeout('Custom timeout', 'CUSTOM_TIMEOUT');
case 'internal':
  throw ToolError.internal('Custom internal', 'CUSTOM_INTERNAL');
```

Remove the stale comment block (lines 5-11). The import can stay as-is since it already imports ToolError.
